annotations.label: Euler characteristic (polyhedra)

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic was classically defined for the surface of a three-dimensional polyhedron

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal notion of (combinatorial) polyhedron with vertex/edge/face counts, so the classical V−E+F definition is not present.

annotations.label: Euler's polyhedron formula

anchor.snippet: Any three-dimensional convex polyhedron 's surface has an Euler characteristic of

annotations.note: No polyhedron API in Mathlib; the V−E+F=2 statement is not stated or proved.

annotations.label: Cayley's modified formula for regular polyhedra

anchor.snippet: Arthur Cayley derived a modified form of Euler's formula using the density

annotations.note: No notion of star polyhedron or polygon/vertex-figure density in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projective and toroidal polyhedra

anchor.snippet: Projective polyhedra all have Euler characteristic 1

annotations.note: Projective/toroidal polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of plane graphs

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic can be defined for connected plane graphs by the same

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks any plane/planar graph face structure, so V−E+F for plane graphs is not defined.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a connected plane graph

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic of any plane connected graph G is 2

annotations.note: Mathlib has no planar/plane-graph theory and so does not state V−E+F=2.

annotations.label: Cauchy's proof of Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: It applies to any convex polyhedron, and more generally to any polyhedron whose boundary is topologically equivalent to a sphere

annotations.note: Cauchy's combinatorial proof scheme is not present, as the underlying objects (polyhedra/triangulations of spheres) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a finite CW-complex

anchor.snippet: for any finite CW-complex, the Euler characteristic can be defined as the alternating sum

annotations.note: Mathlib has CW complexes (Mathlib.Topology.CWComplex) but no Euler-characteristic definition counting cells by dimension.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: for a simplicial complex , the Euler characteristic equals the alternating sum

annotations.note: Although Mathlib has SimplicialComplex/SimplicialSet, no Euler characteristic counting k-simplices with sign is defined.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic via Betti numbers

anchor.snippet: for any topological space , we can define the n th Betti number

annotations.note: Betti numbers of topological spaces are not formalized in Mathlib; only the chain-complex variant (HomologicalComplex.homologyEulerChar) exists.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic (differentiable)

anchor.snippet: For an oriented, compact smooth manifold without boundary

annotations.note: Mathlib's smooth-manifold library has no Euler-characteristic definition via de Rham cohomology or Poincaré–Hopf.

annotations.label: Homotopy invariance of Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: It follows that the Euler characteristic is also a homotopy invariant

annotations.note: Without a topological Euler characteristic in Mathlib, its homotopy invariance is not stated.

annotations.label: Contractible space has Euler characteristic 1

anchor.snippet: any contractible space (that is, one homotopy equivalent to a point) has trivial homology

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have an Euler characteristic for topological spaces, so χ(point)=1 is not stated.

annotations.label: Convex polyhedron surface

anchor.snippet: any convex polyhedron is homeomorphic to the three-dimensional ball

annotations.note: No formal connection between convex polyhedra and the 2-sphere/Euler characteristic exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Additivity under disjoint union

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic of their disjoint union is the sum of their Euler characteristics

annotations.note: No topological-space Euler characteristic exists in Mathlib to state χ(X⊔Y)=χ(X)+χ(Y).

annotations.label: Inclusion–exclusion for Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic obeys a version of the inclusion–exclusion principle

annotations.note: Without a topological Euler characteristic, the χ(A∪B)=χ(A)+χ(B)−χ(A∩B) formula is not present.

annotations.label: Failure of inclusion–exclusion

anchor.snippet: A counterexample is given by taking X to be the real line

annotations.note: Not formalized: requires a topological Euler characteristic, which Mathlib does not provide.

annotations.label: Quotient space formula

anchor.snippet: If X is a finite CW-complex and A is a subcomplex, then

annotations.note: χ(X/A)=χ(X)−χ(A) for CW pairs is absent; Mathlib has CW subcomplexes but no Euler characteristic.

annotations.label: Connected sum formula

anchor.snippet: For two connected closed n -manifolds

annotations.note: Connected sum of manifolds is not defined in Mathlib, so the χ formula is absent.

annotations.label: Product property

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic of any product space M × N is

annotations.note: A product formula χ(α×β)=χ(α)χ(β) exists for bounded finite posets, but the topological version for product spaces/manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Covering space formula

anchor.snippet: for a k -sheeted covering space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Euler characteristic of a covering space; χ(Ẽ)=k·χ(E) is not stated.

annotations.label: Riemann–Hurwitz formula

anchor.snippet: for a ramified covering space , the Euler characteristic of the cover can be computed from the above

annotations.note: The Riemann–Hurwitz formula is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibration product property

anchor.snippet: the fibration is orientable over a field K, then the Euler characteristic with coefficients in the field K satisfies the product property

annotations.note: Multiplicativity of χ for orientable fibrations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transfer map composition

anchor.snippet: For fiber bundles, this can also be understood in terms of a transfer map

annotations.note: Transfer maps for fiber bundles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Football Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: It is common to construct footballs by stitching together regular pentagons and hexagons

annotations.note: Combinatorial polyhedra and their face counts are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: The n -dimensional sphere has singular homology groups equal to

annotations.note: The singular homology computation of Sⁿ giving χ(Sⁿ)=1+(−1)ⁿ is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real projective space Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: The n -dimensional real projective space is the quotient of the n -sphere by the antipodal map

annotations.note: Euler characteristic of ℝPⁿ is not formalized; the homology of real projective spaces is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Torus Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: The n -dimensional torus is the product space of n circles

annotations.note: χ(Tⁿ)=0 is not stated; Mathlib has no Euler characteristic for topological spaces.

annotations.label: Closed odd-dimensional manifold has Euler characteristic 0

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic of any closed odd-dimensional manifold is also 0

annotations.note: Poincaré duality and its corollary χ(M)=0 for closed odd-dim manifolds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus formula for closed orientable surfaces

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic of a closed orientable surface can be calculated from its genus

annotations.note: No classification of closed surfaces or notion of genus in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-orientable genus formula

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic of a closed non-orientable surface can be calculated from its non-orientable genus

annotations.note: Non-orientable genus is not defined in Mathlib; χ=2−k formula absent.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic equals Euler number

anchor.snippet: For closed smooth manifolds, the Euler characteristic coincides with the Euler number

annotations.note: Neither the Euler number of the tangent bundle nor its identification with χ is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss–Bonnet theorem

anchor.snippet: For closed Riemannian manifolds , the Euler characteristic can also be found by integrating the curvature

annotations.note: The Gauss–Bonnet theorem is not formalized in Mathlib (no hits for Gauss-Bonnet).

annotations.label: Descartes' theorem on total defect

anchor.snippet: A discrete analog of the Gauss–Bonnet theorem is Descartes' theorem

annotations.note: Descartes' total angular defect theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hadwiger's theorem

anchor.snippet: Hadwiger's theorem characterizes the Euler characteristic as the unique

annotations.note: Hadwiger's classification of valuations on convex bodies is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a combinatorial cell complex

anchor.snippet: For every combinatorial cell complex , one defines the Euler characteristic as the number of 0-cells

annotations.note: No combinatorial cell complex with Euler characteristic by cell count exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a chain complex

anchor.snippet: one can define the Euler characteristic of any chain complex to be the alternating sum of the ranks of the homology groups

annotations.note: Defined as a `finsum` of `(c.χ i) * Module.finrank R (C.homology i)` for a homological complex of `ModuleCat R`; the underlying `GradedObject.eulerChar` is also exposed.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf

anchor.snippet: For any coherent sheaf

annotations.note: Mathlib has coherent sheaves (Sites.Coherent) but no Euler characteristic via cohomology dimensions for them.

annotations.label: Teardrop orbifold Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: the teardrop orbifold has Euler characteristic

annotations.note: Orbifolds (and the teardrop) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a bounded finite poset

anchor.snippet: The concept of Euler characteristic of the reduced homology of a bounded finite poset

annotations.note: Defined as `mu 𝕜 (⊥ : α) ⊤` for a finite bounded order, matching the Möbius-function definition described in the article.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a finite category

anchor.snippet: This can be further generalized by defining a rational valued Euler characteristic for certain finite categories

annotations.note: Leinster's rational Euler characteristic for finite categories is not formalized in Mathlib.
